title: hieracium bifidum subsp. rhogaleum
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. rhogaleum, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective against wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium bifidum subsp. rhogaleum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and regulating bowel movements, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
